Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn VARCHAR(13) PRIMARY KEY, title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,  author VARCHAR(255)  );   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn VARCHAR(13), serial_number INT PRIMARY KEY, weight INT NOT NULL, bookcase INT, FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author)  VALUES ('1234567890', 'Book Title 1', 'Author 1');   INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author)  VALUES ('1234567890', 'Book Title 2', 'Author 2');

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION total_library_weight_limit CHECK (     (SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy) <= 1000000000 );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn INT,      title TEXT,     author TEXT,      PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn INT,     serial_number INT NOT NULL,     weight INT,     bookcase INT,      PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number),      FOREIGN KEY (isbn)      REFERENCES Book (isbn)         ON UPDATE CASCADE         ON DELETE CASCADE  );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: -- Enable foreign key constraints (for SQLite) PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;   -- Create the Book table CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY,              -- Constraint 1: isbn must be unique     title TEXT NOT NULL,                -- A book must have a title     author TEXT                         -- Author can be NULL if not specified );   -- Create the Copy table CREATE TABLE Copy (     serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,  -- Each copy has a unique serial number     isbn TEXT NOT NULL,                 -- Copy must be associated with a valid book     weight INTEGER NOT NULL,            -- Weight in grams     bookcase INTEGER,                   -- Bookcase can be NULL if unspecified     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)          ON DELETE CASCADE               -- Constraint 4: Remove all copies if the book is deleted         ON UPDATE CASCADE               -- Constraint 3: Update isbn for copies if book isbn changes );

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: -- Add a CHECK constraint to the Copy table ALTER TABLE Copy ADD CONSTRAINT weight_check CHECK (weight >= 10 AND weight <= 1000);

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: -- The assertion to ensure that there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' ASSERT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM Book b     JOIN Copy c ON b.isbn = c.isbn     WHERE b.author = 'Jennifer Widom' );

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: -- The assertion to ensure total weight of all book copies does not exceed 1 billion grams ASSERT NOT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM Copy c     GROUP BY 1  -- Grouping all rows together (effectively calculating the total)     HAVING SUM(c.weight) > 1000000000  -- 1 billion grams );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: -- The assertion to ensure no bookcase exceeds 1,000,000 grams of books ASSERT NOT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM Copy c     JOIN Book b ON c.isbn = b.isbn     GROUP BY c.bookcase     HAVING SUM(c.weight) > 1000000 );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT); CREATE TABLE Copy(isbn TEXT NOT NULL, serial_number INT NOT NULL, weight INT NOT NULL, bookcase INT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY(isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE);

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;   CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY,     title TEXT NOT NULL,      author TEXT );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn TEXT,     serial_number INTEGER,     weight INTEGER NOT NULL,     bookcase INTEGER NOT NULL,     PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number);     FOREIGN KEY (isbn)         REFERENCES Book(isbn)         ON DELETE CASCADE         ON UPDATE CASCADE );

The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn TEXT NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn));   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn TEXT NOT NULL, serial_number INT NOT NULL, weight INT NOT NULL, bookcase INT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE);

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book(isbn, title, author) VALUES ('12345', 'Book 1', 'Author 1'); INSERT INTO Book(isbn, title, author) VALUES ('12345', 'Book 2', 'Author 2');

Answer: UPDATE Book SET isbn = '9781292025821' WHERE isbn = '9781292025827';

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = '9781292025827';

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn TEXT NOT NULL, serial_number INT NOT NULL, weight INT NOT NULL CHECK (weight > 10 AND weight < 1000), bookcase INT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE);

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION jennifer_exists CHECK ( EXISTS (SELECT * FROM Book B JOIN Copy C ON B.isbn = C.isbn WHERE B.author = 'Jennifer Widom'));

Answer: CREATE ASSERTION Total_Copies_Weight_Limit CHECK ((SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy) <= 1000000000);

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,     title VARCHAR NOT NULL,     author VARCHAR );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn VARCHAR,     serial_number VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,     weight INTEGER NOT NULL,     bookcase INTEGER NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)         ON DELETE CASCADE         ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn VARCHAR(13),  PRIMARY KEY (isbn),  title TEXT NOT NULL,  author TEXT);    CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn VARCHAR(13),  serial_number INT NOT NULL,  weight INT NOT NULL,  bookcase INT NOT NULL,  PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)   ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE);

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book(isbn, title, author) VALUES ('0123456789876', 'title1', 'author'); INSERT INTO Book(isbn, title, author) VALUES ('0123456789876', 'title2', 'author');

A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY,      title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,     author VARCHAR(255)          ); CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn VARCHAR(255),           serial_number INT NOT NULL,        weight INT CHECK (weight > 0),     bookcase INT,                    PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number),      FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)         ON UPDATE CASCADE                 ON DELETE CASCADE       );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author) VALUES ('9781234567890', 'First Book Title', 'First Author'); INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author) VALUES ('9781234567890', 'Second Book Title', 'Second Author');

Answer: UPDATE Book SET isbn = '1111111111111' WHERE isbn = '9781292025827';

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = '9781292025827';

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn CHAR(13),     serial_number INT,     weight INT CHECK (weight BETWEEN 10 AND 1000),     bookcase INT,     PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number),     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION AuthorJenniferWidomCopyExists CHECK (     EXISTS (         SELECT *         FROM Book, Copy WHERE Book.isbn = Copy.isbn AND         Book.author = 'Jennifer Widom'     ) );

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION TotalWeightUnderLimit CHECK (     (SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy) <= 1000000000 );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION BookcaseWeightLimit CHECK (     NOT EXISTS (         SELECT 1         FROM Copy         GROUP BY bookcase         HAVING SUM(weight) > 1000000     ) );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (isbn TEXT, PRIMARY KEY(isbn), UNIQUE (isbn), title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT NOT NULL); CREATE TABLE Copy(isbn TEXT, serial_number INT, UNIQUE(serial_number), weight INT, bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY(isbn, serial_number) , FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE);

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn VARCHAR(15) PRIMARY KEY,     title VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,     author VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL );     CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn VARCHAR(15),     serial_number INT,     weight INT CHECK (weight > 0),     bookcase INT,     PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number),     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)          ON UPDATE CASCADE         ON DELETE CASCADE );

The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(    isbn VARCHAR(13),    title VARCHAR NOT NULL,    author VARCHAR,     PRIMARY KEY(isbn) ); CREATE TABLE Copy(    isbn VARCHAR(13), serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT,     PRIMARY KEY(isbn, serial_number),    FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE NOT DEFERRABLE );

Answer: INSERT INTO Book(isbn, title, author) VALUES ('9781292025827, 'Database Systems-course for beginners ', 'Ioana Ofrim'); INSERT INTO Book(isbn, title, author) VALUES (9781292025827', 'Database Systems advanced ', 'Isabela Negoita');

Answer: UPDATE Book SET isn = 9781292025822' WHERE isbn = 9781292025827;

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = '9781292025822';

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy(    isbn VARCHAR(13), serial_number INT, weight INT,    CHECK (weight >= 10 AND weight <= 1000),    bookcase INT,    PRIMARY KEY(isbn, serial_number),    FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE NOT DEFERRABLE  );

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION library_size СHECK(1000000000 >= (SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy));

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION bookcase_size CHECK (   ALL (     SELECT SUM(weight)     FROM Copy     GROUP BY bookcase   ) <= 1000000 );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY,       title TEXT NOT NULL,            author TEXT                         );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn TEXT NOT NULL,                    serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,     weight INTEGER NOT NULL,             bookcase INTEGER NOT NULL,              FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)         ON UPDATE CASCADE                       ON DELETE CASCADE              );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn VARCHAR(13),  title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn VARCHAR(13),  serial_number INT,  weight INT, bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: Create table Book( isbn int Primary key, title text not null, author text); Create table Copy(isbn int, serial_number int primary key, weight int,bookcase int,  Foreign key (isbn) references Book(isbn)) on delete set null on update set null;

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION Jennifer_Wisdom_included CHECK(        EXISTS (SELECT *         FROM Copy C        JOIN Book B ON C.isbn = B.isbn        WHERE B.author = 'Jennifer Wisdom') );

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION Total_Weight_Limit CHECK (     (SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy) <= 1000000000 );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION Bookcase_Weight_Limit CHECK (     NOT EXISTS (         SELECT bookcase         FROM Copy         GROUP BY bookcase         HAVING SUM(weight) > 1000000     ) );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn CHAR(13) PRIMARY KEY,     title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,     author VARCHAR(255) );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn CHAR(13),     serial_number INT NOT NULL,     weight INT NOT NULL,     bookcase INT NOT NULL,     PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number),     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)         ON UPDATE CASCADE         ON DELETE CASCADE );

Answer: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION enforce_total_weight_limit() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$ BEGIN     IF (SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy) > 1000000000 THEN         RAISE EXCEPTION 'Total weight of book copies exceeds 1 billion grams.';     END IF;     RETURN NEW; END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;   CREATE TRIGGER check_total_weight AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON Copy FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE FUNCTION enforce_total_weight_limit();

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE TRIGGER check_bookcase_weight BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON Copy FOR EACH ROW BEGIN     SELECT CASE         WHEN (SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy WHERE bookcase = NEW.bookcase) + NEW.weight > 1000000         THEN RAISE(ABORT, 'Total weight of books in the bookcase exceeds 1,000,000 grams')     END; END;

Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;   CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY,     title TEXT NOT NULL,     author TEXT NOT NULL );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn TEXT,     serial_number     weight INTEGER NOT NULL,     bookcase INTEGER NOT NULL,     PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number),     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)          ON DELETE CASCADE         ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION always_jennifer_widom_copy CHECK ( EXISTS (    SELECT *    FROM Book B, Copy C    WHERE B.isbn=C.isbn    AND B.author='Jennifer Widom'  ) );

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION max_billion_grams CHECK(     ( SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy )<=1000000000 );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION max_bookcase_million_grams CHECK ( NOT EXISTS (         SELECT bookcase         FROM Copy         GROUP BY bookcase         HAVING SUM(weight) > 1000000     ) );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: Book(isbn, title, author)     Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)   CREATE TABLE Book (    isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY,    title TEXT NOT NULL,    author TEXT                           -- can be null if no author is known );   CREATE TABLE Copy (    serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,    isbn TEXT NOT NULL,    weight INTEGER NOT NULL,    bookcase INTEGER NOT NULL,    FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book (isbn)       ON DELETE CASCADE       ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Answer: CREATE ASSERTION written_by_widom CHECK (EXISTS ( SELECT author FROM Copy WHERE author = 'Jennifer Widom');

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION limit_weight CHECK ((SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy) =< 1000000000);

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION limit_weight_case CHECK (NOT EXISTS (SELECT bookcase FROM Copy WHERE (SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy GROUP BY bookcase) > 1000000;

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;   CREATE TABLE Book( isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT);   CREATE TABLE Copy (serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, isbn TEXT NOT NULL, weight INTEGER NOT NULL,bookcase INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book (isbn)ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book (isbn,title,author) VALUES ('978-3-16-148410-0', 'Oliver Twist', 'Charles Dickens'); INSERT INTO Book (isbn,title,author) VALUES ('978-3-16-148410-0', 'War and Peace', 'Leo Tolstoy');

Q Text: Let's assume that the book table contains the following row (and no other rows):     ('9781292025827', 'A first course in Database Systems', 'Jennifer Widom') Give an SQL statement that violates constraint 2.
Answer: INSERT INTO Copy (serial_number, isbn, weight, bookcase) VALUES (1, '1234567890123', 500, 101);

Q Text: Let's assume that we have two copies of Jennifer Widom's book, i.e., the table Copy has the following two rows:    [ ('9781292025827', 1, 200, 81),      ('9781292025827', 2, 200, 81) ] Give an SQL update statement that shows that condition 3 is handled correctly.
Answer: UPDATE Book SET isbn = '9780321188390' WHERE isbn = '9781292025827';

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = '123456789'; SELECT * FROM Copy WHERE isbn = '123456789';

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy ( serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, isbn TEXT NOT NULL, weight INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (weight >= 10 AND weight <= 1000), bookcase INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES BOOK (isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE);

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: CREATE TRIGGER CheckAuthor BEFORE INSERT ON Copy FOR EACH ROW BEGIN     SELECT COUNT(*)     INTO author_exists     FROM Book     WHERE author = 'Jennifer Widom'; END;

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE TRIGGER CheckTotalWeight BEFORE UPDATE ON Copy FOR EACH ROW BEGIN     DECLARE total_weight BIGINT;     SELECT SUM(weight) INTO total_weight FROM Copy;     IF (total_weight - OLD.weight + NEW.weight) > 1000000000 THEN         SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Total weight of book copies in the library cannot exceed 1 billion grams';     END IF; END;

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE TRIGGER CheckBookcaseWeight BEFORE INSERT ON Copy FOR EACH ROW BEGIN     DECLARE total_weight BIGINT;     SELECT SUM(weight) INTO total_weight     FROM Copy     WHERE bookcase = NEW.bookcase;     IF (total_weight + NEW.weight) > 1000000 THEN         SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Total weight of books in the bookcase cannot exceed 1,000,000 grams';     END IF; END;
